evidence.description: Coal Miners coming up a Coal Mine Elevator after a day of work in 1920's Belgium This picture speaks a thousand words. No matter what anybody says, we’re living in the best time in the history of human mankind. We’re eating better, we’re living longer and we’re safer than ever. We ought to be grateful for everything that we have today, people in the past did not have these privileges. | 391 comments on LinkedIn
evidence.text:
Coal Miners coming up a Coal Mine Elevator after a day of work in 1920's Belgium This picture speaks a thousand words. No matter what anybody says, we’re living in the best time in the history of human mankind. We’re eating better, we’re living longer and we’re safer than ever. We ought to be grateful for everything that we have today, people in the past did not have these privileges.
We have unions to thank for better working conditions!
It’s nice to be reminded of how much we actually have sometimes! Showing gratitude daily always leaves me in a better mood.
Well, as you might know, some European countries are urgently thinking about returning back to coal due to the lack of natural gas. Will we be seeing this again?
Spoken like someone who has had the benefit of wealthy white male privilege his entire life.
Men always have the easy jobs!!! Lol
here is the comment (social interaction) ur aiming for.... once cheap, forever cheap!
Sie nennen es Privileg, ich nenne es Menschenwürde und Menschenrechte. Dafür sollten wir alle nicht dankbar sein, sondern wie all die Vorfahren, die für die Veränderung gekämpft haben, sollten Wir auch dafür kämpfen. Es gibt leider vieler Orte, wo diese Bedingungen wie auf dem Bild noch herrschen.
Thanks for posting (Hard working people) ❤️
Please remember this picture and be sure we doesn't waste electricity...

Coal Miners coming up a Coal Mine Elevator after a day of work in 1920's Belgium. Source : Unknown 🤷🏻♂️ Credit for original content© Disclaimer : No copyright intended All rights and credits reserved to respective owner (s) DM or tag us in the comments for credits/removal
Wow!
That is incredible!
My Dad and both Grandfathers worked here. Italians that moved to Belgium for free housing and a job to provide for their families. I wish I had recorded their stories for my Grandson.
Jusque dans les années 1960. Et la catastrophe du cazier en 56 a été provoquée par une cage.
Can't imagine that people will do anything to survive
Mining Health and Safety has come a long way. The slogan "Safety First" emanates from such caged congested "human loads" of the past. Need not mention the fatalities during these era's. Technology and general awareness of safety procedures has changed the current extraction of minerals. Such a capture 'photograph' is a classic treasure which needs to be shared to the current crop of miners. Mr Preventive : Maintenance and Safety News thumb's up
Thank goodness for MSHA & OSHA. Those were scary times!
Esto simplemente se llama "esclavitud" seguro que los dueños de esas minas, pasaron a la historia como buenos emprendedores que daban trabajo (de comer), esto es en Bélgica pero no difiere en nada a los demás países, VERGÜENZA
I think the point is every major mining law is made after a significant number of fatalities, humble hooks, kepps, conveyance slack rope devices, second means of egress, protection from inrush, all together pointed at needing engendered mines, run by professionals, now with the closure of many mining schools - the future of the profession that built the world is in many respects like that conveyance (never cage)
Thats bad indeed packed like sardines eish OMG.
